mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid acceleration in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions that precede corporate revenue contraction, causing equity investors to price in lower earnings and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment acceleration signal, or the signal shows no predictive power for earnings revisions.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP growth accelerates while consumer credit simultaneously expands, it indicates synchronized demand-side momentum that boosts corporate revenue visibility, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline GDPC1-only model in out-of-sample backtests, or forward returns turn negative when credit expands alongside stagnant GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts act as a leading indicator for aggregate demand; when housing construction falls below 2000 thousand units while GDP growth remains positive, it signals a structural deceleration in consumer spending that drags down equity valuations over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when housing starts decline below the threshold during periods of strong GDP growth, indicating housing is no longer a binding demand constraint.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in the industrial production index reflects sustained capacity utilization and supply-side resilience, which supports corporate operating leverage and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The index trend shows no correlation with forward equity returns during periods of supply chain disruption, or Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in core consumer prices forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy stances, compressing equity risk premiums and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite monthly CPI spikes above the threshold, or the signal fails to predict subsequent Fed policy tightening announcements.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts confirms broad-based economic momentum that reduces recession probabilities, prompting institutional investors to increase equity allocations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns when labor and housing data diverge, or the interaction effect disappears after controlling for GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real consumer credit indicates tightening household balance sheets that directly suppress near-term retail sales, causing immediate equity repricing and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during periods of high leverage.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns remain positive when consumer credit contracts, or the signal shows no predictive power for retail earnings surprises.
